title: Medical Emergency - Unconscious Caller
description: 911 receives a pocket dial from a phone left in a pocket or bag. The background audio suggests a medical emergency: heavy, labored breathing or gasping, possibly indicating a stroke, heart attack, or overdose. The caller is unresponsive to dispatcher prompts.

states.initial: The call connects with background sounds of labored breathing or gasping. No verbal response from caller.
states.listening: Dispatcher hears continuous labored breathing with occasional gasps. No coherent speech.
states.escalating: The breathing becomes more erratic, then silent. Possible medical crisis.
states.intervention: Dispatcher sends emergency services based on address trace. Police/medics arrive.
states.resolved: Medical help arrives; caller is treated or transported.

initial_variations: [Heavy, labored breathing sounds]
[Gasping for air, then silence for 10 seconds]
[Faint moaning, then loud snoring-like noise]
[Irregular breathing, occasional cough]

ASK_LOCATION.description: Dispatcher asks for the caller's location

initial: 911, what's the address of your emergency? *silence* Can you tell me where you are?
I need your location. Please speak or tap if you can hear me.
listening: I can hear you're having trouble breathing. Where are you located? *no answer*
Please try to make a sound if you need help. I need your address.
escalating: Caller, stay with me! I need your location! *breathing stops*
If you can hear me, press any button or make a noise to confirm your location.
intervention: Dispatch has your approximate location. Paramedics are on the way.
I have your address from the ping. Help is coming.
resolved: Location has been identified. Units are en route.

ASK_DETAILS.description: Dispatcher asks what happened or symptoms

initial: Can you tell me what's wrong? Are you injured? *labored breathing*
What happened? Are you alone? *no response*
listening: I can hear you're in distress. Can you describe your symptoms?
If you can't speak, try to tap the phone twice for yes.
escalating: Caller, stay conscious! Can you open your eyes? *silence*
Keep breathing! Help is almost there.
intervention: Paramedics have been briefed on the breathing pattern.
Details are limited. Medical team is prepared for cardiac arrest.
resolved: Emergency medical services have arrived at the scene.

ASK_CALLER_STATUS.description: Dispatcher checks if caller is conscious

initial: Caller, if you can hear me, say 'yes' or make a sound. *breathe*
listening: I'm not getting a response. I'm going to send help.
Stay on the line. I'll send an ambulance immediately.
escalating: Caller? *no response* I'm dispatching all available units.
Loss of consciousness suspected. Alerting paramedics.
intervention: Medics are breaking into the residence. Stand by.
Police are on scene to assist.
resolved: Caller has been found and is receiving care.

initial: If you're choking, try to cough. I'm sending help.
If you can, lie on your back and keep the line open.
listening: I'm going to guide you through CPR if someone is with you.
Keep the phone near your mouth, I'm listening for changes.
escalating: Don't move the caller if there's a fall. Wait for paramedics.
Try to keep the airway clear.
intervention: Help is there. Let the professionals take over.
You've done well. The team is arriving.
resolved: Instructions no longer needed. Emergency services are handling.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived but medical is needed. Delay in care.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters are not trained for this medical emergency. Paramedics needed.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrived and provided life-saving treatment. Caller stable.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control dispatched instead of medical. Caller died before help arrived.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as pocket dial. Caller found dead hours later.

title: Domestic Dispute - Open Line
description: A pocket dial from a phone in a purse or pocket captures a heated domestic argument escalating into physical violence. The caller is likely a victim who cannot speak openly. Background includes pleading, thuds, and breaking glass. Dispatcher must deduce the situation and send help.

states.initial: Call connects with loud arguing and emotional distress.
states.hiding: Sounds of someone moving away, whispering, or suppressed crying.
states.confronting: Aggressive voices, yelling, and sounds of struggle.
states.escalating: Violence intensifies - thuds, screams, objects breaking.
states.secured: Police arrive, shouting 'Police! Drop it!' then silence or compliance.

initial_variations: You never listen! I'm tired of this! *slap*
Please, stop! It hurts! *crying*
Get away from me! I'll call the police! *phone ruffling*
You think you can just walk out? *door slam*

specific_details: I'm sorry, I didn't mean it

initial: 911, do you need help? *arguing continues* I need your address.
If you can hear me, stay on the line. Where is this happening? *no response*
hiding: I hear someone hiding. Give me the address, and help will come quietly.
If you can't speak, press any key. I'll trace your location.
confronting: Stay calm. I need to know where you are to send police.
I can hear the situation is tense. I need your location.
escalating: Tell me your address! I'm sending help right now.
If you can, whisper the address. We'll be there.
secured: Location has been confirmed. Police are en route or on scene.

initial: What's going on? Is someone hurt? *shouting*
Can you tell me what happened? *pleading in background*
hiding: I hear crying. Is someone injured? Make a sound if yes.
If you're in danger, tap the phone once for yes.
confronting: I hear yelling. Is anyone threatening you? *aggressive voice*
Stay on the line. I'm noting details.
escalating: I hear a struggle. I'm sending police immediately.
If you can, describe the weapon or situation.
secured: Police have arrived. The situation is being contained.

initial: Caller, are you safe to talk? *no answer* I'm sending help.
If you're in danger, say nothing. I'll stay on the line.
hiding: I know you're there. Stay hidden until police arrive.
Don't make a sound. Help is coming.
confronting: I hear the conflict escalating. I'm dispatching now.
Stay on the line. Do not intervene.
escalating: Caller, if you can hear me, try to get to a safe place.
Police will be there in minutes. Hold on.
secured: You are safe now. Police have the situation under control.

initial: If you can, move to a room with a lock. I'm staying on the line.
Try to stay calm. Help is on the way.
hiding: Good, you're hidden. Keep the phone on mute if needed.
Do not confront the abuser. Police will handle it.
confronting: Please try not to provoke. I'm tracking the call.
If you can, put the phone down and step away.
escalating: I hear violence. Police are breaking into the home.
If you're being attacked, scream as loud as you can.
secured: Help has arrived. You are not alone anymore.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived during the assault and arrested the abuser. Victim is safe.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrived but were not trained for domestic violence. Abuser fled and victim was severely injured.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medics arrived without police presence and were met with hostility. Abuser attacked medics.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control dispatched due to mistaken assumption of animal noise. Victim died from injuries.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as a hang-up or prank. Victim was later found deceased from domestic violence.

title: The Shifting Cargo
description: Open line from a moving truck. Muffled cries and banging from the back.

states.initial: Caller's pocket dials 911 while driving a truck.
states.hearing_cries: Dispatcher hears muffled voices and banging from cargo area.
states.confrontation: Sounds of struggle and shouting; truck stops.

initial_variations: Hello? This is 911. Can you hear me? (loud engine noise, faint banging)
911, what is your emergency? (static, muffled thumping, a distant voice saying 'help')
You've reached 911. Please state your emergency. (truck engine, clanking, then a child's cry)

initial: I can't speak right now, but something's wrong in the back.
Just driving, but I hear... wait, what's that?
I didn't mean to call, but you hear that?
hearing_cries: It's getting louder! I think it's a kid!
The banging won't stop. I'm scared.
confrontation: Who are you?! Get back! (sounds of struggle)
He's got a weapon! I'm pulling over!
They broke the lock! Help!

initial: I'm on the I-95 northbound, near exit 15.
Just passed the old factory on Maple.
I don't know, I'm a driver for ABC Movers.
hearing_cries: We're on a rural road, lots of trees. I see a sign for Smithville.
I've stopped at a warehouse off Route 9.
I'm at a truck stop, but I don't know the name.
confrontation: I'm at the abandoned mall parking lot! Hurry!
Corner of Elm and 3rd! He's getting away!
I don't know! It's dark!

initial: Just me in the cab. But there's something in the back.
I think there might be multiple people back there.
I heard at least two voices.
hearing_cries: At least one child, maybe more. And an adult yelling.
Two people: one crying, one threatening.
Sounds like a woman and a kid.
confrontation: The driver and someone from the back. They're fighting!
Two men, one with a gun!
I see three people, they're arguing.
silence: I think they took one person.
There's a body on the ground.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived and intercepted the truck. Rescued 3 victims of human trafficking. Suspects arrested.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire department arrived but no fire. They assisted police in breaking into the cargo area.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medical team treated victims for dehydration and minor injuries.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrived to find human victims. Not appropriate. Delayed rescue.

DISMISS.message: Dispatched as pocket dial. Victims remained trapped for hours until another call came in.

title: The Argument in the Garage
description: Pocket dial from a garage where a heated argument escalates to a crash.

states.initial: Pocket dial; sounds of arguing in background.
states.escalation: Shouting, then a loud crash, followed by crying.
states.confrontation: One voice becomes threatening; sounds of struggle.

initial_variations: Hello? 911? (shouting in background: 'You never listen!')
Service connected. (sounds of an argument, a woman yelling 'Stop!')
You've reached emergency. (loud male voice: 'I said get back here!')

initial: I didn't mean to call but... they're fighting again.
My phone must have dialed. Should I hang up?
It's my parents... they argue like this all the time.
escalation: He just threw something! She's crying!
I think he hit her! I hear a crash!
It's getting worse! Should I intervene?
confrontation: He's yelling 'I'll kill you'! I'm scared!
She's screaming 'put the knife down'!
There's a struggle, furniture breaking!
silence_after: It's quiet now... oh no, she's not moving.
I think he left. She's just lying there.
I can hear moaning... please send help.

initial: I'm at home. 456 Cherry Blossom Lane.
In the garage at the Smith house, end of the street.
I don't know, I just moved here.
escalation: Same house, the one with the red door.
It's the corner house on 5th and Elm.
123 Maple, I think. The garage is open.
confrontation: 456 Oak, hurry! He's got a weapon!
I'm at the blue house on Hill street, garage door open.
I can see the address: 789 Pine.
silence_after: Still here, 456 Oak. No one's speaking.
I think they're both in the garage at 123 Cherry.
I hear a car starting. They might be leaving.

initial: Two people: a man and a woman.
Three people: a couple and a child.
I just hear two voices: one deep, one shrill.
escalation: It's a man and a woman. Maybe a teen too?
Definitely two adults, one sounds older.
I think the woman is pregnant.
confrontation: Same two, but now the man is threatening with a knife.
The woman is on the floor, man standing over her.
I see a child hiding in the corner.
silence_after: I heard a door slam, maybe one left.
I think the woman is unconscious.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived to find a domestic assault in progress. Suspect arrested, victim taken to shelter.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrived, no fire. They called police and provided first aid.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance treated the victim for bruises and shock. Police took report.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrived to find a domestic dispute. Not helpful; police called later.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as pocket dial. Victim suffered further injuries before neighbor called.

title: The Playground Accident
description: Pocket dial from a parent's pocket while child is playing; then a scream and thud.

states.initial: Call connects; sounds of children playing.
states.accident: Loud thud and child screaming in pain.
states.panic: Parent's voice is heard panicking, calling child's name.
states.medical_care: Parent tries to calm child, asks for help.

initial_variations: 911? (laughing children, swing set creaking)
Emergency line. (kids shouting, ball bouncing)
You've reached 911. (parent humming, child saying 'watch me!')

initial: We're just playing. Everything is fine.
accident: Oh no! Ethan! (loud thud) He fell!
He's not moving! Lily, wake up!
I heard a crack! I think his arm is broken!
panic: My son! He's bleeding! Please help!
Someone call 911! I can't get him to respond!
I can't carry him! He's too heavy!
medical_care: I think he's in shock. He's pale.
He's breathing, but his leg looks bent wrong.

initial: At the playground behind the community center.
The park near the library, I think.
accident: Same place! The big slide area!
Near the swings at the school playground.
panic: The playground at 5th and Park! Hurry!
It's the wooden playground in Riverside Park.
I'm at the one with the blue slides.
medical_care: Still here, near the monkey bars.
I haven't moved. Please come quickly.
Address is 100 Park Ave, playground in back.

panic: Just me and my injured child.
My wife is here too, she's frozen.
Only me and him. Please hurry.
medical_care: Still just us. Some other parents are coming.
My other kids are watching, scared.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived but it was a medical emergency. They stabilized child until ambulance arrived.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters responded, no fire. Helped comfort child while waiting for medics.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance arrived quickly. Child had a broken arm and concussion; treated successfully.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrived, confused. Child delayed getting medical help.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as pocket dial. Child's injury worsened before another parent called 911.

title: The Break-in Next Door
description: Pocket dial from a neighbor's open line; glass breaking and footsteps.

states.initial: Open line with ambient noise of a home.
states.intrusion: Sound of breaking glass, then footsteps.

initial_variations: 911, what is your emergency? (TV sound in background, dog barking)
Hello? You've reached 911. (sounds of a conversation, then a crash)
This is 911. (a loud smash, then - 'What was that?')

initial: Just sitting at home. Nothing wrong.
intrusion: Someone just broke in! Through the back door!
There's someone in the house! Footsteps!
confrontation: They're in the next room! I hear voices!
It sounds like a struggle! A man yelling!
Oh god, they're coming up the stairs!
aftermath: It's quiet now. I think they left.
I heard a door slam. They might be gone.
I'm too scared to check. Please come.

initial: It's the blue house on the corner.
I'm at my home, 5678 Pine Lane.
intrusion: I'm at 1234 Maple. The door is broken!
Same address, but the break-in is next door!
Wait, it's not my house? It's next door!
confrontation: I'm hiding in my closet at 1234 Maple!
The noise is from the house next to me, 1236 Maple.
I'm at 5678 Pine, but the break-in is across the street.
aftermath: Still at my house. I think they're gone.
I see a car speeding away from 1234 Maple.
They went into the house at 1236 Maple.

initial: My family is here, we're watching TV.
intrusion: I think there's more than one intruder.
At least two voices. One sounds angry.
I only heard one set of footsteps.
confrontation: I heard a woman scream. Maybe the homeowner.
The intruders are fighting with someone!
I think the homeowner is confronting them.
aftermath: I think the homeowners are safe? I don't know.
I hear crying. Maybe someone got hurt.
It's quiet now. Could be over.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived in time to catch the burglars exiting. Homeowner safe, suspects arrested.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrived; no fire. They secured the scene until police came.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance arrived; no injuries. Police took over.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to a break-in; inappropriate. Burglars escaped.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as pocket dial. Homeowner was robbed at gunpoint; later called from a neighbor's phone.

title: The Stolen Phone
description: A stolen phone connects to 911; you hear the thief talking about committing a crime.

states.initial: Call connects; background conversation about a 'job'.
states.planning: Thieves discuss details of a planned robbery.
states.execution: Sounds of a robbery in progress (alarm, yelling).
states.getaway: Sounds of running, car starting, talk of escape.

initial_variations: 911? (someone says: 'Yeah, I got the gun, meet at the bank.')
Emergency line. (voice: 'We hit the jewelry store at noon, be ready.')
You've reached 911. (muffled: 'I snagged this phone, we'll ditch it later.')

initial: I can't speak. They're planning a crime.
This phone was stolen! They're talking about a robbery.
planning: They said the heist is at 3pm.
They're casing a house on Elm Street.
They have a map and are discussing entry points.
execution: Alarm! I hear a shot! They're robbing the store!
Someone is screaming! The thieves are inside!
getaway: Engines revving. They're fleeing the scene.
I hear them laughing and counting money.
They're splitting up. One is heading east.

initial: They're in a car. I hear road noise.
I think they're near the downtown area.
Passing by a sign that says 'City Bank'.
planning: They parked near the corner of 5th and Main.
The house they're watching is 123 Oak.
They're at the jewelry store in the mall.
execution: We're at the First National Bank on Broad Street!
Inside the mall, near the food court.
The house on 123 Oak. I hear a door crashing.
getaway: They're driving on the highway, heading north.
Just turned onto Route 9. Blue sedan.
They're pulling into a warehouse on Industrial Dr.

initial: I think three people are involved.
Just one thief talking on the phone.
planning: Two in the car, one waiting at the location.
Four guys total. Two inside, two lookouts.
A group of three. One seems to be the leader.
execution: I hear multiple voices inside the bank.
The thieves and some hostages, I think.
They're yelling at someone. Might be a guard.
getaway: Only two in the car now. The others split.
I think one was injured. They left him.
They're all in the car now, speeding away.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police tracked the phone and intercepted the thieves. Robbery prevented, suspects arrested.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire responded; no fire. They reported suspicious activity to police but delayed response.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance arrived; no injuries. Police arrived late; thieves escaped.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to a crime scene. Inappropriate response; robbers got away.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as pocket dial. Thieves successfully robbed the location and escaped.

title: The Fight Behind the Muffled Call
description: A pocket dial reveals a domestic violence incident in progress. The caller cannot speak openly, but background sounds indicate a physical altercation.

states.initial: Caller is silent or whispering, background sounds of arguing and hitting.
states.hiding: Caller has moved to a safer location, still unable to speak freely.
states.confronting: The abuser has noticed the call and is confronting the victim.
states.secured: Police have arrived and the scene is under control.

initial_variations: Whispering: 'Please... don't hurt me...' with sounds of crying and thuds.
Silent call with voices arguing: 'You never listen!' followed by a slap.
Muffled: 'I'm sorry, I'm sorry!' and a door slamming in the background.
Breathing heavily, then a male voice: 'Who are you calling?' and a struggle.

specific_details: a child crying in the background

hiding: He's looking for me... please hurry to {address_location}.
confronting: He knows I called! {address_location}!
He's taking the phone... {address_location}...
No! Please, I'm at {address_location}!
He's coming!

hiding: I think he has a weapon, {specific_details}.
confronting: He's got a {specific_details}!
No! Don't! {specific_details}!
He's pulling my hair, {specific_details}!
I can't breathe, {specific_details}!

hiding: I hear him in the hallway.
confronting: He found me!
Help! He's breaking the door!
He's yelling at me now.

SEND_POLICE.message: Officers arrived and arrested the abuser. Victim is safe.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrived but no fire. Police had to be called separately, delaying response.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medical crew treated minor injuries, but police were needed for the assault.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to a domestic violence scene. Victim was further endangered.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as pocket dial. Victim suffered severe injuries.

title: The Crash on the Highway
description: An open line from a pocket dial reveals a serious car accident. The caller is disoriented and in pain.

states.initial: Caller groaning, sounds of traffic and metal.
states.trapped: Caller is stuck in the vehicle.
states.rescued: Emergency services have extracted the caller.

initial_variations: Groaning: 'My leg... it hurts...' with car horn in background.
Labored breathing and moaning: 'Help... I'm stuck...'
Whispered: 'I can't move...' followed by car alarms.
Coughing: 'There's smoke... please...'

initial: Near {address_location}... I can see the sign.
trapped: Still at {address_location}. I can't get out.
rescued: I'm being loaded into ambulance at {address_location}.
evacuated: I'm at the hospital now, not {address_location}.

initial: I think I'm okay but stuck.
trapped: The car is starting to catch fire!
evacuated: I'm safe now, thanks to you.
Everyone is out of the car.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived quickly but medical was needed first. Minor delay.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire department arrived and extinguished a small fire, but medical and police were also required.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance treated the injured caller and transported to hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrived at a crash scene. Victim bled out waiting for medical.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as pocket dial. Victim died from injuries.

title: The Toddler's Toy Phone
description: A child accidentally dials 911 while playing with a phone. Background shows cartoons and childish giggles.

states.initial: Child babbling, TV playing in background.
states.parent_intervenes: Parent or guardian takes the phone.

Babbling: 'Wheels on the bus...' and toy noises.
Singing: 'Baby shark doo doo...'
Laughing: 'Peekaboo!' followed by a TV jingle.

parent_intervenes: Sorry, this is {address_location}. My child dialed.
She was playing with my phone, {specific_details}.
explained: Already said, it's a child with a phone, {specific_details}.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police responded to a child playing with phone. Wasted resources.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire engine dispatched for a toddler's toy. Reckless use of emergency services.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Ambulance sent for a child's accidental call. No medical need.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control showed up for a child's play call. Highly inappropriate.

DISMISS.message: Call correctly identified as accidental and dismissed. No resources wasted.

title: The Heist Conversation

states.initial: Two voices discussing plans, sounds of maps or typing.
states.suspicious: They sense something wrong and check the phone.
states.aborted: They abort the plan due to suspicion.

Voice 1: 'Remember, no witnesses. We cut the cameras first.'
Voice 2: 'We need a getaway driver. What about Joe?'

suspicious: We're still at {address_location} but something's wrong.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police intercepted the criminals based on intel. Robbery prevented.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire trucks arrived at a non-fire scene. Alerted the suspects, who escaped.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medical crew responded to a planned heist. No medical emergency, suspects fled.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control showed up to a robbery planning. Suspects panicked and committed more crimes.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as pocket dial. The heist succeeded, with casualties.

title: The Toddler's Toy Phone

states.initial: Silence, background sounds of intruders

initial_variations: (silence, muffled footsteps and voices)
Please, just take what you want (whisper)
(heavy breathing, then a door slams)

title: The Medical Episode
The Shaky Walk
description: An elderly person's phone accidentally dials 911 from their pocket. Background audio captures heavy breathing, shuffling footsteps, then a loud crash followed by prolonged silence except for faint moaning. The caller is unresponsive, suggesting a fall with potential injury.

states.initial: Caller is unresponsive, background audio only.
states.listening: Dispatcher attempts to gather clues from ambient sounds.
states.probed: Dispatcher asks questions, no verbal response, but audio changes (e.g., movement).
states.resolved: Dispatch decision made and outcome determined.

initial_variations: [Sound of heavy breathing, shuffling, then a loud thud followed by silence]
[Breathing followed by a distant crash, then soft whimpering]
[Muffled footsteps, a clatter, then the sound of something heavy hitting the floor, then nothing]
[Audio of a door creaking, then a sudden fall with a groan, then shallow breathing]

You hear only the sounds described. No location given.

listening: You hear a faint television in the background, possibly a talk show.
There's a clock ticking steadily.
A dog barks occasionally in the distance.
probed: After asking, you hear a slight shift in the background, perhaps someone moving.
No verbal response, but the breathing pattern changes.
The moaning seems to increase in volume momentarily.
resolved: Location was determined via triangulation or neighbor call.
Address was found through previous records.

initial: No verbal response. Only ambient sounds.

The crash sounded like a heavy object falling.
probed: You ask 'Can you hear me?' – no response, but the breathing becomes more ragged.
You ask 'Are you hurt?' – no answer, but a faint moan is heard.
resolved: The situation was understood from the audio and subsequent investigation.

initial: No response. The audio doesn't indicate immediate danger beyond the fall.
The line is open but no clear danger cues.
listening: The environment seems quiet except for the caller's breathing. No signs of an intruder.
No loud noises indicate ongoing threats.
probed: No changes in background audio suggest the scene is safe or not.
The caller does not respond, so safety is unknown.
resolved: Safety assessment completed based on available info.

initial: The caller is unresponsive to verbal prompts.
listening: You hear faint moaning, suggesting semi-consciousness.
Breathing is present but irregular.
probed: You ask 'Can you squeeze my hand?' – no response, but the moaning stops briefly.
The caller seems unable to respond.
resolved: Consciousness status determined from audio and eventual responder report.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived to find an elderly person who had fallen and needed medical attention. Police assisted but medical was more appropriate. Minor deduction for incorrect primary dispatch.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters responded to a medical fall. They provided basic first aid but lacked full medical equipment. Better to send medical. Moderate penalty.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrived quickly, assessed the fall victim, provided treatment, and transported to hospital. The correct dispatch for a medical emergency.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to a medical emergency. They were unequipped and delayed proper care. The caller's condition worsened. Severe penalty.

DISMISS.message: You dismissed the call as a pocket dial. The elderly person lay injured for hours before a neighbor found them. Life-threatening delay.

title: The Garage Argument
description: A phone in someone's pocket accidentally dials 911 during a heated argument in a garage. Background audio: shouting, then a struggle, a loud bang (like a gunshot or heavy tool hitting), then an abrupt end with footsteps running. The caller does not respond.

[Muffled shouts, a thud, then a distinct gunshot sound, then nothing]

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived to find a domestic violence victim with a head injury from a fall. Suspect fled but was later apprehended. Correct dispatch for potential violent crime.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrived but found no fire. They provided first aid until paramedics arrived. Inefficient use of resources.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Medical dispatched but police also needed for safety. Medical team entered an unsafe scene. Minor coordination issue.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to a violent domestic incident. They were unable to intervene and the victim's condition deteriorated. Extremely inappropriate dispatch.

DISMISS.message: You dismissed the call as a pocket dial. The victim suffered severe injuries and the suspect was not caught. Life-threatening mistake.

title: The Cargo Hold
description: A warehouse worker's phone accidentally dials 911 while in a pocket. Background audio: machinery sounds, then a loud crash, then a muffled voice calling for help, saying 'I'm trapped under a pallet!' The caller cannot speak directly due to being pinned.

states.initial: Open line with industrial sounds and a distant plea.
states.listening: Dispatcher hears the trapped victim's calls.
states.probed: Dispatcher attempts communication; voice responds faintly.

initial_variations: [Machinery hums, then a loud clatter, then a voice: 'Help! I'm stuck!']
[Sound of a forklift, a crash, then a muffled: 'Somebody help!']
[Conveyor belt noise, then a shattering, then a faint: 'I can't move!']
[Banging metal, a heavy thud, then a voice: 'I'm under this pallet!']

initial: No verbal location from caller, but background suggests a warehouse.
The voice is faint and doesn't provide an address.
listening: You hear echoes typical of a large open space like a warehouse.
Forklift beeps indicate an industrial area.
probed: You ask 'Where are you?' – the voice responds: 'Warehouse A, near the loading dock!'
After asking, you hear a faint: 'I'm in the main storage area!'
resolved: Location determined from the voice response or GPS.

initial: The crash sounds like a heavy object falling. The voice says they're trapped.
You hear machinery then a collapse.
listening: The voice says 'I'm trapped under a pallet' - confirms injury.
You can hear the victim's breathing and occasional cries.
probed: You ask 'What happened?' – the voice: 'A pallet fell on me! I can't get out!'
You ask 'Are you injured?' – the voice: 'My leg is pinned!'
resolved: Details obtained: trapped under pallet, leg injury.

initial: The environment is hazardous - falling objects possible.
listening: No further crashing sounds, but heavy machinery still running.
The scene is unstable; additional collapses could occur.
probed: You advise to stay still; the victim responds they can't move anyway.
No indication of immediate further danger beyond the initial accident.

initial: The voice indicates the caller is conscious but trapped.
listening: Voice continues to call for help, conscious.
Breathing is labored but conscious.
probed: You ask 'Can you stay with me?' – voice: 'Yes, but it hurts.'
Victim responds to questions, alert.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrived to find a trapped warehouse worker. They secured the scene and called for medical and fire rescue. Not the primary needed dispatch, but helpful for scene security.

SEND_FIRE.message: Fire rescue arrived with lifting equipment and freed the worker from under the pallet. Correct dispatch for a trapped victim with potential crush injury.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrived and provided immediate medical care once the victim was freed. Good dispatch but fire rescue needed for extrication. Acceptable.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control responded to an industrial accident. They were useless, and the victim's condition worsened due to delay. Severe penalty.

DISMISS.message: You dismissed the call as a pocket dial. The trapped worker died from crush syndrome hours later. Catastrophic failure.
